description: Implemented UCB1 scoring mode in dispatch_optimizer.py. Compared heuristic (12+ constants) vs UCB1 (1 parameter) on ranking, score distribution, and coverage properties.
expect: UCB1 (c=1.414) + 20% cold-domain floor replaces current scoring. Score Gini decreases >30%. Coverage uniformity improves.

actual.key_finding: Score uniformity != visit uniformity. Heuristic has LOWER score Gini (0.178 vs 0.299) but visits WORSENED (Gini 0.459→0.827 over 10 sessions). UCB1 concentrates scores on under-explored domains BY DESIGN, which drives uniform visits over time. The 4 rounds of heuristic patches optimized the wrong target.

diff: Predicted Score Gini decreases >30% — WRONG, UCB1 score Gini is 68% HIGHER. But this is correct behavior: UCB1 achieves visit uniformity through score NON-uniformity (exploring under-visited arms). Did NOT predict the score-uniformity/visit-uniformity inversion. Score spread reduction confirmed (87.7%). Top-10 overlap only 3/10 — more radical than expected. Heuristic structural bias (meta #1 with 11L/5B/7ISO) confirmed as root cause of visit concentration.
next_steps: Trial: use --mode ucb1 for 10 sessions (S376-S385), then re-measure visit Gini
If visit Gini improves by >10pp, make UCB1 the default and delete heuristic constants
Consider Thompson sampling (Tier 2) if UCB1 shows non-stationarity issues
